root_text.sn22.158:1.2: “Kismiṁ nu kho, bhikkhave, sati, kiṁ upādāya, kiṁ abhinivissa uppajjanti saṁyojanābhinivesavinibandhājjhosānā”ti?
root_text.sn22.158:1.4: “rūpe kho, bhikkhave, sati, rūpaṁ upādāya, rūpaṁ abhinivissa uppajjanti saṁyojanābhinivesavinibandhājjhosānā.
root_text.sn22.158:1.8: viññāṇe sati, viññāṇaṁ upādāya, viññāṇaṁ abhinivissa uppajjanti saṁyojanābhinivesavinibandhājjhosānā.
root_text.sn22.158:1.13: api nu taṁ anupādāya uppajjeyyuṁ saṁyojanābhinivesavinibandhājjhosānā”ti?

translation_text.sn22.158:1.2: “Mendicants, when what exists, because of grasping what and insisting on what, do fetters, insistence, shackles, and attachments arise?”
translation_text.sn22.158:1.3: “Our teachings are rooted in the Buddha. …”
translation_text.sn22.158:1.4: “When form exists, because of grasping form and insisting on form, fetters, insistence, shackles, and attachments arise.
translation_text.sn22.158:1.8: consciousness exists, because of grasping consciousness and insisting on consciousness, fetters, insistence, shackles, and attachments arise.
translation_text.sn22.158:1.13: “But by not grasping what’s impermanent, suffering, and perishable, would fetters, insistence, shackles, and attachments arise?”
translation_text.sn22.158:1.16: They understand: ‘… there is nothing further for this place.’”
